text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PATIENT: Raymond Kowalski
MRN: [REDACTED]
ACCESSION NUMBER: SP-[REDACTED]
ATTENDING SURGEON: [REDACTED]
ATTENDING PATHOLOGIST: [REDACTED]
REFERRING PHYSICIAN: [REDACTED]
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

CLINICAL HISTORY:
66-year-old male (now 67 at time of surgery) with borderline resectable pancreatic head adenocarcinoma (cT3N1M0, Stage III at diagnosis). Prior EUS-guided FNA confirmed ductal adenocarcinoma. Patient received neoadjuvant modified FOLFIRINOX (8 cycles) followed by consolidative long-course chemoradiation (IMRT 50.4 Gy in 28 fractions with concurrent capecitabine). Post-neoadjuvant restaging demonstrated significant radiographic and biochemical response (CA 19-9 decline from 1200 to 62 U/mL; tumor size reduction from 3.8 cm to 2.1 cm). Now presenting for definitive surgical resection. Intraoperative frozen section of pancreatic neck margin was negative.

PROCEDURE DATE: [DATE REDACTED — TO BE ADDED PROGRAMMATICALLY]

TYPE OF PROCEDURE:
Open pancreaticoduodenectomy (Whipple procedure) with segmental superior mesenteric vein resection and primary end-to-end reconstruction.

SPECIMEN(S) SUBMITTED:
  A. Pancreaticoduodenectomy specimen (en bloc): pancreatic head and uncinate process, duodenum with proximal jejunum, gallbladder, distal common bile duct
  B. Regional peripancreatic and periportal lymph nodes (separately submitted by surgeon)
  C. Segmental superior mesenteric vein (SMV) resection segment
  D. Intraoperative frozen section — pancreatic neck margin (result communicated intraoperatively: NEGATIVE FOR MALIGNANCY)

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
GROSS DESCRIPTION
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

SPECIMEN A — PANCREATICODUODENECTOMY SPECIMEN:
Received fresh and subsequently fixed in 10% neutral-buffered formalin is a pancreaticoduodenectomy specimen consisting of the pancreatic head and uncinate process, a segment of duodenum, proximal jejunum, gallbladder, and distal common bile duct. The specimen is oriented by the operating surgeon with sutures marking the SMA (superior mesenteric artery) groove margin.

Overall specimen dimensions: 12.5 x 7.2 x 5.0 cm.

Pancreas: The pancreatic head and uncinate process measure 6.5 x 5.0 x 3.5 cm. The external pancreatic surface is smooth to focally nodular with areas of fibrosis. The cut surface of the pancreatic parenchyma reveals a 2.0 x 1.8 x 1.6 cm ill-defined, firm, white-tan, hypocellular-appearing mass in the head of the pancreas with extensive surrounding fibrosis and areas of grossly apparent necrosis and yellow-tan discoloration, consistent with treatment effect. The mass is centered in the head of the pancreas and extends toward the uncinate process. The main pancreatic duct upstream (body/neck) measures approximately 4 mm in diameter (mildly dilated). Parenchyma outside the tumor bed appears atrophic and fibrotic, consistent with prior ductal obstruction. No satellite nodules identified.

Margins (per standardized axial slicing technique, Leeds protocol-based):
  - Pancreatic neck (proximal) margin: tumor grossly NOT at margin; closest gross distance approximately 1.5 cm.
  - Anterior (peritoneal-covered) surface: tumor NOT at margin grossly; closest gross distance approximately 2 mm.
  - Posterior margin (retroperitoneal/SMA groove margin): tumor NOT at margin grossly; closest gross distance approximately 1-2 mm (sections submitted for histologic verification).
  - SMV groove / portal vein groove margin: inked separately in blue; tumor grossly NOT at this margin; closest distance approximately 1 mm.
  - Common bile duct margin (proximal): grossly uninvolved.

Duodenum: 25 cm segment of duodenum and 5 cm proximal jejunum. The duodenal mucosa is intact and unremarkable. The ampulla of Vater is identified and shows patency, with an indwelling plastic biliary stent in situ (stent intact, submitted separately as clinical note). The biliary stent lumen is patent.

Common bile duct / hepatic duct margin: The distal common bile duct is 2.5 cm in length with a diameter of up to 1.2 cm (mildly dilated, consistent with prior biliary decompression stenting). Wall shows mild fibrosis.

Gallbladder: 8.5 x 3.5 cm. Serosa smooth and glistening. Wall thickness 3 mm. Mucosa shows coarse granularity. No stones identified. No mass lesions.

Representative sections submitted: 12 cassettes (full axial cross-sections through tumor mass, with color-coded margins: anterior margin — red ink, posterior/SMA groove margin — black ink, SMV groove margin — blue ink, pancreatic neck margin — separately submitted frozen section recut in permanent section).

SPECIMEN B — REGIONAL LYMPH NODES:
Received in multiple labeled containers:
  - "Peripancreatic" nodes: multiple fibrofatty tissue fragments, 4.0 x 2.5 x 1.5 cm in aggregate; 8 lymph node candidates identified, sizes 0.3–1.5 cm.
  - "Periportal" nodes: fibrofatty tissue, 3.0 x 2.0 x 1.0 cm in aggregate; 6 lymph node candidates, sizes 0.2–0.9 cm.
  - "Pericholedochal" nodes: fatty tissue, 2.5 x 1.8 x 1.0 cm; 4 lymph node candidates.
  All tissue entirely submitted.

SPECIMEN C — SMV RESECTION SEGMENT:
Received is a segment of superior mesenteric vein measuring 2.2 cm in length x 0.8 cm in diameter. Outer adventitial surface is inked. Cross-sections show patent vascular lumen with wall thickening and fibrosis at one pole. No gross tumor thrombus. Submitted entirely.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
INTRAOPERATIVE CONSULTATION (FROZEN SECTION)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Specimen D — Pancreatic Neck Margin (frozen section, communicated intraoperatively):
Frozen section diagnosis: NEGATIVE FOR MALIGNANCY. No carcinoma identified at the pancreatic neck transection margin. No high-grade PanIN (PanIN-3) at margin. Result communicated to attending surgeon intraoperatively.
Permanent section of frozen recut: CONFIRMED — No carcinoma at margin. Pancreatic parenchyma with chronic pancreatitis-like changes and mild ductal ectasia. No PanIN-3.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
DIAGNOSTIC FINDINGS — HISTOLOGIC EXAMINATION
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:

A. PANCREATICODUODENECTOMY (WHIPPLE) SPECIMEN — PANCREATIC HEAD AND UNCINATE PROCESS:

1. PRIMARY TUMOR:
   MODERATELY DIFFERENTIATED DUCTAL ADENOCARCINOMA, PANCREATOBILIARY TYPE
   - Histologic Grade: G2, moderately differentiated
   - Tumor Size (residual viable carcinoma): 2.0 cm in greatest dimension
   - Tumor Location: Head of pancreas / uncinate process
   - Histologic Description: Sections through the tumor mass demonstrate moderately differentiated infiltrating ductal adenocarcinoma arranged in irregular glands, tubular structures, and occasional cribriform nests embedded within a dense desmoplastic stroma. Tumor cells show moderate nuclear pleomorphism, vesicular chromatin, variably prominent nucleoli, and moderate amounts of pale eosinophilic cytoplasm. Luminal mucin is focally present. Perineoplastic fibrosis is prominent and accounts for the majority of the lesional volume. Large areas of acellular hyalinized fibrosis, ghost gland remnants, mucin pools without viable epithelium, foamy histiocyte accumulations, cholesterol clefts, and hemosiderin deposition are present, consistent with extensive treatment effect (necrosis and therapy-induced regression). Viable tumor nests are scattered throughout the fibrotic bed, representing residual carcinoma after neoadjuvant therapy.

2. TREATMENT RESPONSE — TUMOR REGRESSION GRADE:
   COLLEGE OF AMERICAN PATHOLOGISTS (CAP) TUMOR REGRESSION GRADE: Grade 2
   (Moderate response: Residual cancer with evident and extensive treatment effect; acellular fibrosis/necrosis accounting for approximately 60–65% of the tumor bed; viable carcinoma present but markedly reduced compared to expected pre-treatment tumor burden)
   Ryan/CAP Grade 2 — Partial pathologic response. Not meeting criteria for near-complete (Grade 1) or complete (Grade 0) pathologic response.

3. SURGICAL MARGINS (all assessed by perpendicular and radial sections):
   - Pancreatic neck (proximal) margin: NEGATIVE FOR CARCINOMA — clearance > 10 mm (R0)
   - Anterior (peritoneal/serosal) margin: NEGATIVE FOR CARCINOMA — closest approach 2.1 mm (R0)
   - Posterior margin / SMA groove (retroperitoneal margin): NEGATIVE FOR CARCINOMA — closest approach 1.5 mm (R0)
   - SMV/portal vein groove margin: NEGATIVE FOR CARCINOMA — closest approach 1.0 mm (R0)
   - Common bile duct (proximal hepatic duct margin): NEGATIVE FOR CARCINOMA
   - Distal enteric/jejunal margin: NEGATIVE FOR CARCINOMA
   OVERALL RESECTION STATUS: R0 (MICROSCOPICALLY COMPLETE RESECTION — all margins free of carcinoma)

4. REGIONAL LYMPH NODES:
   3 of 18 lymph nodes POSITIVE for metastatic adenocarcinoma (pN1)
   - Peripancreatic nodes: 2/8 positive (largest metastatic deposit 0.9 cm; extranodal extension NOT identified in these nodes)
   - Periportal nodes: 1/6 positive (largest metastatic deposit 0.5 cm; extranodal extension present — focal, < 1 mm beyond capsule)
   - Pericholedochal nodes: 0/4 negative
   Total: 3/18 lymph nodes positive
   NOTE: Positive nodes show treatment effect with areas of necrosis within the metastatic deposits, consistent with partial response to prior neoadjuvant chemotherapy.

5. PERINEURAL INVASION: PRESENT (extensive; seen at multiple levels of the tumor)

6. LYMPHOVASCULAR INVASION: PRESENT (small vessel lymphovascular invasion identified in tumor-adjacent parenchyma)

7. VENOUS (LARGE VESSEL) INVASION: PRESENT — Carcinoma identified in the wall of the segmental SMV resection specimen (see Specimen C); adventitial involvement without intraluminal extension. See SMV segment below.

8. PANCREATIC INTRAEPITHELIAL NEOPLASIA (PanIN):
   Low-grade PanIN (PanIN-1/2) identified in non-neoplastic pancreatic parenchyma remote from main tumor. No PanIN-3 (high-grade) at any margin.

9. NON-NEOPLASTIC PANCREATIC PARENCHYMA:
   Moderate to marked chronic pancreatitis-like changes including lobular atrophy, acinar loss, interlobular and intralobular fibrosis, and ductal ectasia — consistent with chronic obstructive pancreatitis secondary to ductal adenocarcinoma.

10. DUODENUM AND AMPULLA OF VATER:
    Ampulla of Vater: Unremarkable; no dysplasia or adenoma identified. Biliary stent tract site shows reactive mucosal changes. Duodenal mucosa: Mildly reactive, no adenoma, no carcinoma involvement.

11. COMMON BILE DUCT:
    Wall with mild fibrosis and chronic inflammation, consistent with prior stenting and obstructive effects. No carcinoma identified.

B. GALLBLADDER:
   Chronic cholecystitis with mild mucosal atrophy. No carcinoma, no dysplasia, no stones.

C. SUPERIOR MESENTERIC VEIN RESECTION SEGMENT:
   Cross-sections show fibrosis and mild mural thickening of the venous wall. No intraluminal tumor thrombus. Adventitial tissue demonstrates DESMOPLASTIC CARCINOMA INFILTRATION consistent with direct extension from the pancreatic primary — carcinoma cells present in the adventitia and outer media; intimal surface uninvolved. This finding is incorporated in the pathologic T classification below.

D. SEPARATELY SUBMITTED LYMPH NODES — see above (incorporated into Specimen B reporting).

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
PATHOLOGIC STAGING (AJCC 8th EDITION — POST-TREATMENT / ypTNM)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

  ypT:   ypT3   (Tumor involves the superior mesenteric vein wall; adventitial carcinoma present in SMV segment; residual tumor 2.0 cm)
         NOTE: Revised from initial ypT2 assessment after review of SMV specimen — adventitial involvement qualifies as extrapancreatic extension into peripancreatic soft tissue/adjacent vessel wall consistent with ypT3.
  ypN:   ypN1   (3/18 regional lymph nodes positive)
  ypM:   ypM0   (no distant metastasis identified in resection specimen; clinical/radiographic staging informing M status)

  PATHOLOGIC STAGE (POST-NEOADJUVANT): ypT3N1M0 — Stage IIA–IIB (AJCC 8th edition)
  RESECTION STATUS: R0

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
ANCILLARY STUDIES
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed on formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded tumor sections):

  CK7:        POSITIVE (diffuse, strong) — consistent with pancreatobiliary adenocarcinoma
  CK20:       NEGATIVE — argues against colorectal primary
  CDX2:       NEGATIVE — argues against intestinal/colorectal differentiation
  MUC1:       POSITIVE (apical and cytoplasmic) — pancreatobiliary type
  MUC2:       NEGATIVE
  MUC5AC:     POSITIVE (focal, moderate) — consistent with ductal adenocarcinoma
  CA19-9 (IHC, tumor cells): POSITIVE
  SMAD4 (DPC4):  LOSS OF EXPRESSION (complete absence of nuclear and cytoplasmic staining in tumor cells; internal positive control — stromal and vascular endothelial cells — intact) — consistent with homozygous SMAD4 deletion identified on prior molecular profiling; associated with increased risk for systemic dissemination
  p53 (IHC):  DIFFUSE STRONG NUCLEAR OVEREXPRESSION (aberrant "gain-of-function" pattern, > 80% of nuclei strongly positive) — consistent with TP53 p.R248W hotspot mutation identified on prior NGS
  Ki-67 (MIB-1): Approximately 25% of viable tumor cells positive (moderate proliferation index, reflecting post-treatment state)
  MLH1:       RETAINED (intact nuclear expression)
  MSH2:       RETAINED (intact nuclear expression)
  MSH6:       RETAINED (intact nuclear expression)
  PMS2:       RETAINED (intact nuclear expression)
  Mismatch repair status: PROFICIENT (MMR-P) — consistent with prior molecular MSS designation

MOLECULAR/OTHER STUDIES:
  KRAS Mutation (IHC surrogate and prior molecular confirmation): KRAS p.G12V mutation confirmed on prior NGS (VAF 42%); IHC for RAS-pathway activity not routinely performed but molecular data on file.
  HER2 (ERBB2): IHC SCORE 1+ (weak, incomplete, basolateral membrane staining in < 10% of tumor cells) — NOT amplified; consistent with prior NGS borderline copy number result (2.8). FISH for ERBB2 amplification: NOT INDICATED based on IHC 1+ score.
  PD-L1 (22C3 antibody, combined positive score [CPS]): CPS 2 (low expressors; < 5% — not a predictor of immunotherapy benefit, consistent with MSS status and prior CPS 1 on FNA specimen).

PENDING STUDIES:
  - BRCA1/BRCA2 germline reflex testing (previously recommended per NCCN; results pending from Clinical Genetics; to be correlated with adjuvant therapy planning)
  - ATM germline testing (pending)
  NOTE: Somatic BRCA1/BRCA2 status already assessed as wild-type on prior NGS panel (FNA specimen); germline testing pending as separate laboratory submission.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
COMMENT
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

This pancreaticoduodenectomy specimen demonstrates moderately differentiated pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma with extensive treatment-related fibrosis and necrosis in the tumor bed following neoadjuvant FOLFIRINOX chemotherapy and chemoradiation. The histomorphologic findings are consistent with CAP tumor regression grade 2 (moderate response), indicating significant but incomplete pathologic response to multimodality neoadjuvant therapy.

All surgical margins are free of carcinoma (R0 resection). Residual viable carcinoma measures 2.0 cm. Regional nodal metastases are present (3/18 nodes), with treatment effect also evident within the positive nodal deposits. Perineural invasion and lymphovascular invasion are identified. Adventitial involvement of the resected SMV segment is noted without intraluminal extension.

IHC confirms loss of SMAD4/DPC4 expression and aberrant p53 overexpression, consistent with the mutational profile identified on prior EUS-FNA molecular testing (KRAS G12V / TP53 p.R248W / SMAD4 homozygous deletion / CDKN2A homozygous deletion). Mismatch repair proteins are intact (MMR-proficient), consistent with microsatellite stable designation. HER2 is not amplified.

Germline BRCA1/2 and ATM testing results are pending and will be reported as an addendum upon receipt.

Results discussed with surgical team. Clinical correlation recommended.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Electronically signed by: Attending Pathologist, MD
Department of Anatomic Pathology
Case reviewed by: [REDACTED], Gastrointestinal Pathology Fellow
Addendum pending: Germline BRCA1/2 and ATM testing results
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
